properties.remark: CLINCH COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT SHARED PHOTOS ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF DAMAGED TRAILERS AND SHEDS, ALONG WITH SNAPPED PINE TREES, ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 441 IN CLINCH COUNTY BETWEEN HOMERVILLE AND FARGO. THIS DAMAGE WAS AT THE LOCATION WHERE A TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNATURE WAS DETECTED BY DOPPLER RADAR AT MOODY AIR FORCE BASE AT ABOUT 1,500 FEET. TIME OF DAMAGE BASED ON RADAR DATA.

CLINCH COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT SHARED PHOTOS ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF DAMAGED TRAILERS AND SHEDS, ALONG WITH SNAPPED PINE TREES, ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 441 IN CLINCH COUNTY BETWEEN HOMERVILLE AND FARGO. NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO, WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH, BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AND CROSSED FARGO HIGHWAY (U.S.-441), DAMAGING STRUCTURES AT A HUNTING CAMP. THIS TORNADO OTHERWISE PRODUCED TREE DAMAGE ALONG A PATH JUST SLIGHTLY LONGER THAN ONE HALF MILE, WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AROUND 300 YARDS.
TREES DOWN IN DIXIE UNION. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

START POINT FOR BRISTOL (PIERCE COUNTY) TORNADO SURVEY. NWS JACKSONVILLE STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN PIERCE COUNTY NEAR DOGWOOD DRIVE, WITH DAMAGE CONTINUING EASTWARD NEAR THE TOWN OF BRISTOL ALONG WALKERVILLE ROAD TO THE INTERSECTION WITH EAST HORSESHOE ROAD.
START POINT FOR NORTHEAST CHARLTON COUNTY TORNADO 1/4/23 STORM SURVEY.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE JACKSONVILLE STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO, WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 80 MPH, BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG RIVER ROAD IN THE NORTHEASTERN PART OF CHARLTON COUNTY, OR ABOUT 5 MILES TO THE SOUTHEAST OF THE TOWN OF WINOKUR. A LARGE TREE WAS DOWNED BY THIS TORNADO ACROSS RIVER ROAD.

DELAYED REPORT. A SURVEY CREW HAS ESTIMATED AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ACROSS SOUTHEASTERN COFFEE COUNTY. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH INCLUDED A PORTION OF SHEET METAL ROOF TORN FROM A BARN ALONG WITH SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES. THE 3.66 MILE PATH OF THE TORNADO BEGAN AT TWENTYNINE MILE CREEK AND ENDED AT GA HIGHWAY 158.

DELAYED REPORT. SURVEY CREWS ESTIMATED AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN BACON COUNTY NORTHWEST OF ALMA GA AND TRACKED 7 MILE NORTHEASTWARD INTO APPLING COUNTY. START TIME AND DURATION WERE APPROXIMATED BY RADAR. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH INCLUDES SEVERAL SNAPPED TREES AND PROPERTY DAMAGE ALONG THE ROAD WHIP POOR WILL WAY.

EM relayed a public report with video and funnel cloud images of a landspout that formed over St. Marys (specifically near 401 E Conyers Street) around 4:17 to 4:22 pm on Monday, July 17th. Two outflow boundaries converged in this area and the resultant storm produced a brief, weak landspout. Radar indicated a brief, weak rotational couplet at 4:22 pm. The video showed broad rotation around a thin, elevated condensation funnel. Debris rotating around the condensation funnel was mostly vegetation. Some objects were blown around, but significant damage did not occur. There was also some wind-blown debris near the Meeting Street Boat Ramp in St. Marys.

An EF0 tornado tracked SE to NW in an outer rainband of Hurricane Idalia across southern Glynn county around 7:30 am. The tornado damaged trees and power lines generally between US Highway 17/US Highway 82 and the South Brunswick River. The path was about 9.78 miles and width about 50 yards.

A tornado moved onshore from the Brunswick River and tracked to the NNW across the westside of the city of Brunswick near New Castle Street where damage to trees and some structures occurred. The tornado continued to track NNW very close to the New Jesup Highway (Highway 341) where additional tree and structure damage occurred. Peak winds were estimated at 90-94 mph making the tornado an EF1.

A short-lived EF1 tornado moved NNW across St. Simons Sound and moved across the southern tier of St. Simons Island in an outer rainband of Hurricane Idalia. Observed damage was to large hard wood trees along a stretch of Kings Way. Maximum width was around 50 yards.

Action News JAX posted a public video of a landspout or a brief tornado touchdown in the vicinity of Lake Asbury. No immediate reports of damage were available. Time of event based on radar data.

This an updated to the previous report of a landspout near Lake Asbury. A witness reported that the spout lasted for 3-5 minutes and tracked WSW to ENE for about 0.75 mile across a construction site. Debris including construction area trash and dust was lifted, but structure damage did not occur. The event occurred just south of Sandridge Road near Feed Mill Road.

The short-lived EF-2 tornado initially touched down near the intersection of Belle Terre Pkwy and Pine Lakes Pkwy. The tornado traveled northeast through the B section of Palm Coast before dissipating near I-95. Several houses sustained significant roof damage in the path, particularly the home along Baltimore Lane which suffered complete destruction of its roof. A minivan was also flipped onto its side and moved from the driveway onto the road along Birchwood Drive. Several screened lanai enclosures, sheds and fences were significantly damaged or destroyed. Several trees were snapped and powerlines were downed.
